cards.title: Koalas Have Fingerprints Almost Identical to Humans
cards.body: Koalas are among the few non-human animals with individualized friction ridges on their hands and feet. Their fingerprint patterns—loops, whorls, and arches—are so similar to human fingerprints that even electron microscope analysis is needed to easily tell them apart. Australian police have even noted that koala prints could theoretically contaminate crime scene evidence.

cards.title: A 25-Foot Wave of Molasses Once Devastated Boston
cards.body: On January 15, 1919, a massive storage tank burst in Boston's North End, releasing over two million gallons of fermented molasses. The resulting wave reached heights of 25 feet and traveled at roughly 35 miles per hour through the streets. It knocked down buildings, trapped horses, killed 21 people, and left the neighborhood smelling sweet for years.

cards.title: A Stray Dog Once Sparked a War Between Two Nations
cards.body: In October 1925, tensions between Greece and Bulgaria escalated into a brief military conflict known as the Incident of Petrich. Historical accounts record that a Greek soldier accidentally ran across the border while chasing his runaway dog. Bulgarian sentries shot the soldier, prompting Greece to invade and occupy the border region until international diplomats negotiated peace.

cards.title: Male Pufferfish Sculpt Underwater Geometric Artwork
cards.body: Male white-spotted pufferfish spend over a week swimming along the seabed, meticulously carving intricate, circular patterns into the sand using their fins. These geometric designs can reach over six feet in diameter. Females inspect the sand circles, and if impressed by the symmetry and fine sand in the center, lay their eggs in the middle of the design.

cards.title: Lemmings Do Not Mass-Suicide Off Sea Cliffs
cards.body: Contrary to popular belief, lemmings do not deliberately march off cliffs to end their lives. The enduring myth was heavily popularized by the 1958 Disney nature documentary White Wilderness, where filmmakers staged a fake lemming mass suicide by driving imported animals off a cliff into a river. In reality, lemmings periodically migrate in large groups and occasionally drown crossing water.

cards.title: This Beetle Uses Infrared Sensors to Hunt Forest Fires
cards.body: While most creatures flee from forest fires, the black fire beetle flies directly toward them. These insects possess specialized pit organs beneath their middle legs equipped with infrared sensory receptors. They can detect the thermal radiation of a forest fire from dozens of miles away, flocking to freshly burned trees to mate and lay eggs without competition.

cards.title: Snails Have Thousands of Microscopic Teeth
cards.body: A garden snail might look completely toothless, but its mouth contains thousands of microscopic teeth. Snails eat using a specialized ribbon-like organ called a radula, which functions like a biological rasp. Depending on the species, a snail can have anywhere from 1,000 to over 20,000 teeth lined up in backward-pointing rows to scrape plant matter or drill through shells.

cards.title: The Pacific Island of Niue Issues Pokemon and Disney Coins
cards.body: The island nation of Niue produces official legal tender collector coins featuring characters like Pikachu, Darth Vader, and Mickey Mouse. Minted in partnership with international mints, these silver and gold coins carry face values in Niue dollars. However, because their precious metal content far exceeds their face value, virtually none are ever spent in local stores.

cards.title: The Lyrebird Can Flawlessly Mimic Chainsaws and Car Alarms
cards.body: Australia's superb lyrebird possesses one of the most complex vocal organs in the bird world. To attract mates and mark territory, lyrebirds mimic not only the calls of dozens of other bird species, but also artificial sounds from human surroundings. Recorded wild and captive lyrebirds have effortlessly reproduced camera shutters, car alarms, fire sirens, and chainsaw engines.

cards.title: Tardigrades Can Survive the Open Vacuum of Space
cards.body: Microscopically tiny tardigrades, often called water bears, can endure extreme conditions that would kill almost any other organism. In 2007, thousands of tardigrades were launched into orbit aboard the FOTON-M3 mission and exposed directly to the vacuum and ionizing solar radiation of open space for 10 days. Upon returning to Earth, many resumed normal life and reproduced.

quizzes.question: Which anatomical feature makes koalas unique among most non-primate animals?
quizzes.explanation: Koalas have friction ridge patterns on their hands that closely resemble human fingerprints.

quizzes.question: How fast did the wave of molasses travel during the 1919 Boston Molasses Flood?
quizzes.explanation: The burst tank unleashed a 25-foot wave of molasses that rushed through streets at roughly 35 mph.

quizzes.question: What incident sparked the 1925 border conflict between Greece and Bulgaria?
options: A soldier chasing a runaway dog across the border
A dispute over offshore fishing rights
A stray cow grazing in a neighboring field
quizzes.explanation: The Incident of Petrich began when a Greek soldier was shot after crossing the border while chasing his dog.

quizzes.question: Why do male white-spotted pufferfish carve geometric sand circles on the seabed?
options: To trap small fish for food
To protect themselves from strong currents
quizzes.explanation: Male pufferfish build elaborate circular sand structures to attract females, who inspect the artwork before laying eggs.

quizzes.question: How did the myth that lemmings commit mass suicide originate?
options: Staged footage in a 1958 Disney documentary
A translation error in an early biology textbook
quizzes.explanation: Filmmakers for Disney's 1958 documentary White Wilderness staged a fake lemming mass suicide by pushing them off a cliff.

quizzes.question: How do black fire beetles locate distant forest fires?
options: By smelling smoke with long antennae
Using infrared sensors located beneath their legs
By listening for crackling wood sounds
Using compound eyes sensitive to UV light
quizzes.explanation: Black fire beetles possess specialized pit organs beneath their legs with infrared sensors that detect heat miles away.

quizzes.question: What is the specialized ribbon-like organ lined with teeth in snails called?
quizzes.explanation: Snails rasp food using a specialized ribbon-like organ covered in microscopic teeth called a radula.

quizzes.question: Why are Niue's pop-culture collector coins rarely spent in retail stores?
options: They are illegal to spend in stores
Their precious metal content far exceeds their face value
Local stores are prohibited from taking coins
They are valid currency only at international airports
quizzes.explanation: The silver and gold metal value in the coins is worth much more than their official face value in Niue dollars.

quizzes.question: What anatomical structure allows the superb lyrebird to mimic complex artificial sounds?
options: Resonant air sacs in its neck
quizzes.explanation: The superb lyrebird has an exceptionally complex syrinx that enables it to imitate complex natural and artificial sounds.

quizzes.question: How long did tardigrades survive direct exposure to open space during the 2007 orbital mission?
quizzes.explanation: Tardigrades survived 10 full days of direct exposure to the vacuum and solar radiation of space aboard FOTON-M3.

guesses.prompt: How fast (in miles per hour) did the wave of molasses travel through the streets of Boston during the 1919 flood?
guesses.explanation: The 2.3-million-gallon tank burst unleashed a 25-foot wave of molasses that rushed through Boston streets at 35 miles per hour.

guesses.prompt: Up to how many microscopic teeth can certain species of snails have on their tongue-like radula?
guesses.explanation: Depending on the species, snails can have thousands or even over 20,000 microscopic teeth arranged in rows along their radula.

guesses.prompt: How many days did tardigrades survive exposed directly to the vacuum and radiation of open space during a 2007 orbital mission?
guesses.explanation: Tardigrades spent 10 days exposed directly to the vacuum and solar radiation of space and survived to reproduce back on Earth.

misconceptions.claim: Koala fingerprints are so similar to human prints that they could potentially confuse forensic investigators.
misconceptions.explanation: Koalas have friction ridges that closely mimic human loops and whorls, making them difficult to distinguish without detailed analysis.

misconceptions.claim: Lemmings deliberately jump off sea cliffs in mass suicidal migrations.
misconceptions.explanation: Lemmings do not commit mass suicide; the famous myth was largely created by staged footage in a 1958 Disney documentary.

misconceptions.claim: Lyrebirds can only imitate the calls of other bird species, not human machinery.
misconceptions.explanation: Lyrebirds frequently mimic man-made sounds in their environment, including car alarms, camera shutters, and chainsaws.
